candidates.title: Order Matters: Empirical and Theory-Grounded Study of Augmentation Operation Sequencing in CNNs and ViTs
candidates.summary: A unified study combining (1) pilot-confirmed empirical evidence that ordering significantly affects accuracy (2.32% spread for ViT/CIFAR-10; 9.01% spread for 6-operation category-level ordering); (2) a revised theoretical framework where the Wasserstein NC_2 measure is tested in feature space (pixel-space NC_2 falsified); (3) a corrected DPI reversibility ranking showing Flip-first (not CJ-first) is the predicted optimal, consistent with the empirical winner Flip→Crop→CJ; and (4) a new H6 (inverted-U magnitude interaction) replacing the falsified H5. Full-scale experiments (200 epochs, 5 seeds) are required before any hypotheses can be formally confirmed or falsified.
hypotheses: H1: Ordering produces >0.3% accuracy spread in ≥2/4 arch-dataset blocks (5-seed paired test, p<0.05)
H2: ViTs show larger absolute ordering sensitivity than CNNs (interaction ANOVA, p<0.05)
H3: Feature-space NC_2 (512-D embeddings, 10k samples) predicts accuracy ranking (Spearman rho>0.5)
H4: Flip-first ordering (corrected DPI prediction) outperforms conventional Crop-first in ≥2/4 blocks
H5b: Interleaved P→G category ordering outperforms all-geometric-first in ≥3/4 blocks
H6: Inverted-U magnitude interaction: M9 spread > M5 and M14 spread (quadratic term significant)
candidates.pilot_focus: Tier 0 DONE (2.68% spread, 6 orderings ResNet-18/CIFAR-10 5k). Full Tier 1 pilot DONE (3/4 blocks >0.5% threshold, 1 seed, 10 epochs). NEXT: Full Tier 1 (5 seeds, 200 epochs, 120 runs) + Tier 2 confirmation pilot (30 epochs, 2 seeds, 2 blocks).
candidates.theoretical_basis: Wasserstein Non-Commutativity bound (bubble-sort decomposition, proof to appear in appendix) + DPI reversibility principle (corrected: Flip-first, not CJ-first, is predicted optimal, consistent with empirical winner)
candidates.key_risk: Effect may shrink at full scale (200ep) due to optimization absorbing ordering differences. Mitigated by paired seed design and pilot evidence showing effects at 10ep/100-sample scale.
candidates.timeline: Tier 0: DONE; Tier 1 full: 10h wall-clock; Tier 2 pilot: 45min; Tier 2 full: 9h; Tier 3 revised: 6h; Tier 4 feature-NC: 1.5h; Total remaining: ~27h wall-clock (4 GPUs)

pilot_verdicts.H4: partially_supported (Flip-first wins 2/4 blocks with corrected prediction)
pilot_verdicts.H5b: preliminary_supported (9.01% spread; 1 seed only; needs confirmation pilot)

critical_warnings: All pilot verdicts require n>=5 seeds and 200 epochs for formal statistical confirmation
H3 was falsified in pixel space with underpowered computation (100 samples in 3072-D); feature-space test with 10k samples in 512-D is qualitatively different
H5b (9.01% spread) from single pilot seed — requires Tier 2 confirmation pilot before full Tier 2 investment
Theorem 1 proof requires bubble-sort decomposition step (deferred to appendix)
DPI contraction coefficient must be shown to depend on input distribution nu (not just channel properties) — theoretical gap acknowledged
sources_of_strength: Pilot shows largest effect in high-spread ViT/CIFAR-10 block (2.32%) — strong signal for full-scale confirmation
Category-level ordering shows 9.01% spread — potentially the most impactful practical finding
Negative results (pixel-space NC_2 falsified, magnitude non-monotonic) are themselves publishable findings
Architecture-differential finding (ViT > CNN sensitivity) is novel and mechanistically grounded
Design is publishable regardless of direction
Zero implementation risk — ordering is just reordering a Python list

candidates.title: Variance Decomposition of Augmentation Pipeline Design Choices
candidates.summary: Instead of asking whether ordering matters, ask how much it matters relative to operation selection, magnitude, and random seed. Full factorial design with 4 independent variables on ResNet-18/CIFAR-10. ANOVA-based variance decomposition. Activation condition: pilot shows spread < 0.2% after full training — very unlikely given current pilot evidence.
hypotheses: Magnitude contributes the largest fraction of accuracy variance (eta-sq > 0.3)
Category-level ordering (geometric vs. photometric) contributes second-largest fraction
Within-category permutation contributes < 5% of total explained variance
Random seed variance is comparable to within-category ordering variance
candidates.pilot_focus: Zero additional training — reuses Tier 1 and Tier 3 runs. Variance decomposition analysis only.
candidates.key_risk: Pilot strongly suggests ordering matters; this backup is unlikely to be activated.
candidates.activation_condition: Full Tier 1 shows ALL blocks with spread < 0.2% in 5-seed experiment

candidates.title: Class-Level Effects of Augmentation Ordering
candidates.summary: Study per-class accuracy effects of ordering, which may be hidden by aggregate accuracy averaging. Zero additional training cost — uses per-class accuracy from Tier 1 CIFAR-100 runs. Should be integrated as a standard secondary analysis section in cand_a paper, not as a standalone candidate.
hypotheses: Per-class ordering sensitivity is significantly larger than aggregate ordering sensitivity
Semantic class groups show consistent directional ordering preferences
Class-level effects are more visible on fine-grained benchmarks (CIFAR-100) than coarse-grained (CIFAR-10)
candidates.pilot_focus: Zero additional training — uses per-class accuracy from Tier 1 runs on CIFAR-100.
candidates.key_risk: CIFAR-100 classes are too coarse to reveal meaningful semantic ordering patterns. Mitigation: group by visual taxonomy.
candidates.activation_condition: Always run as zero-cost secondary analysis on Tier 1 CIFAR-100 data
candidates.differentiation_from_kirichenko: Kirichenko et al. (NeurIPS 2023) vary augmentation strength, not ordering. The independent variable is fundamentally different; must be explicitly stated in related work.

synthesis_notes.front_runner_rationale: Cand_a is the clear front-runner with strong pilot support. The 2.32% ViT ordering effect and 9.01% category-level ordering effect are the largest signals, and the theoretical framework (NC + DPI) provides publication-quality context even with the falsification of pixel-space NC_2 (which is itself a novel finding). The inverted-U magnitude interaction (H6) adds a counterintuitive, memorable result. Cand_b is relegated to a Section in cand_a's paper rather than a standalone candidate. Cand_c is always-run as zero-cost secondary analysis.

perspectives_weighted_this_round.empiricist: Highest — the pilot results dramatically reshape all hypothesis weights. The empiricist's insistence on proper statistical standards (n>=5, paired design, pre-registered thresholds) is the most important correction from iteration 0.
perspectives_weighted_this_round.contrarian: High — the falsification of H3 and H5 validates the contrarian's warnings about small effects and statistical noise. The reflection report's identification of the n=1 problem is a direct vindication of the contrarian's challenge.
perspectives_weighted_this_round.theoretical: High — the DPI reversibility correction (Flip is strictly bijective, not 'medium reversibility') resolves a theoretical inconsistency that was undermining the framework. Feature-space NC_2 replaces pixel-space NC_2 as the theoretical prediction.
perspectives_weighted_this_round.pragmatist: Medium — the 4-GPU parallelization strategy (Tier 1 in 10h wall-clock instead of 40h) is the pragmatist's key contribution this round.
perspectives_weighted_this_round.innovator: Medium — H6 (inverted-U magnitude interaction) is a new, counterintuitive prediction emerging from the pilot data. The feature-space NC_2 hypothesis is a pivot that the innovator would endorse.
perspectives_weighted_this_round.interdisciplinary: Low — the core DPI framework was established in iteration 0; this iteration focuses on empirical refinement.

key_corrections_from_iteration_0: H2 definition: restored to 'architecture differential sensitivity' (not 'reversibility sorting wins')
DPI reversibility: Flip is strictly bijective (eta=1); corrected prediction is Flip-first, consistent with empirical winner
H3: pixel-space NC_2 falsified due to underpowering; replaced by feature-space NC_2 prediction
H5: falsified (not monotonic); replaced by H6 (inverted-U) and H5b (category-level interleaving)
All hypotheses now explicitly labeled 'preliminary' pending full-scale (5 seeds, 200 epochs) confirmation
Theorem 1 proof gap acknowledged (bubble-sort decomposition deferred to appendix)
